title: cyathea villosa
intro: Cyathea villosa, also known as the Hare's Foot Fern or Tree Fern, is a species of tree fern native to Australia, characterized by its distinctive hairy rhizome and erect frond. The plant has been utilized in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Cyathea villosa include its potential to treat skin irritations, wounds, and infections, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Cyathea villosa include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by infusing the leaves or roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
